summary: Matched four previously stubbed HSD synth functions in src/sysdolphin/baselib/synth.c, improving GALE01 matched code by +2304 bytes according to the decomp.dev bot. The PR added implementations for HSD_SynthSFXUpdateMix, HSD_Synth_8038B5AC, HSD_SynthSFXKeyOff, and HSD_Synth_80388E08, plus small supporting type/signature and data-symbol fixes in synth.h, synth.static.h, axdriver, and symbols.txt.

key_files.role: Main matching work; added four synth function implementations and supporting static data/helpers.
Corrected prototypes for stream counters, callbacks, and HSD_Synth_8038B5AC argument types.
Changed HSD_Synth_804D7778 from static s8 to static volatile u8.
Adjusted AXDriver_8038E8EC second parameter from int to u8.
Kept AXDriver_8038E8EC declaration consistent with implementation.
Marked lbl_8040806C as byte data instead of an untyped object.

what_changed: Implemented HSD_Synth_80388E08 as a search through HSD_Synth_804C2AE0 voice/bank lists, unloading and freeing the matching AXVPB when its prev field matches the supplied integer.
Implemented HSD_SynthSFXKeyOff with getNode lookup, driverInactivatedCallback handling, direct voice freeing for flagged nodes, and priority/volume update behavior for normal key-off.

Implemented HSD_SynthSFXUpdateMix, including stereo/mono mix setup through AXPBMIX, panning math using sqrtf_accurate from math_ppc.h, AXSetVoiceMix calls, ITD shift calculation through lbl_8040806C, and AXSetVoiceItdOn/AXSetVoiceItdTarget updates.

Implemented HSD_Synth_8038B5AC for stream playback setup: waits on HSD_Synth_804D7778, disables interrupts, stops/keys off an existing stream node, acquires an AX voice, initializes a synth node, requests stream header data through HSD_DevComRequest, and returns the generated stream node id.
Added supporting static/data objects in synth.c: data_pad, data_pad_2, static AXPBMIX lbl_80407FB4, and byte lookup table lbl_8040806C.
Moved freeVoices earlier in synth.c so it can be reused by HSD_SynthSFXKeyOff before HSD_SynthCallback.
Changed HSD_SynthResetStreamCounters prototype from void(void) to void(int, int, void*, bool), matching its callback-like signature while leaving the visible body unchanged in the excerpt.
Changed HSD_SynthPStreamHeaderCallback declaration to use int, int, void*, bool cancelflag instead of void*, s32, void*, s32.
Changed HSD_Synth_8038B5AC declaration from int, u8, int, int to int, u8, u8, int.
Changed AXDriver_8038E8EC second argument from int to u8 in both axdriver.c and axdriver.h.
Changed HSD_Synth_804D7778 to static volatile u8, consistent with spin-wait usage in HSD_Synth_8038B5AC.
Updated GALE01 symbols.txt so lbl_8040806C is classified as data:byte.
smart_moves: Used exact-width argument types such as u8 and bool in headers to help codegen match call-site extension and callback ABI behavior.
Made HSD_Synth_804D7778 volatile because HSD_Synth_8038B5AC spin-waits on it, preventing the compiler from treating the loop as reading an ordinary stable local/static value.
Recreated data layout with explicit padding arrays and a byte lookup table, allowing data-section ordering and symbol typing to support the code matches.
Introduced small inline helpers, including freeVoices, my_memzero, and HSD_Synth_8038B5AC_inline, to shape codegen while keeping repeated logic understandable.
Used sqrtf_accurate from math_ppc.h for the panning/mix calculations rather than a generic sqrtf, which is important for matching PPC math output.
Preserved an explicit TODO noting that AXVPB prev probably has the wrong struct type, avoiding a silent struct-definition change without enough evidence.
decomp_lessons: For audio code, matching often depends on correcting small integer widths in prototypes; int versus u8 changes generated extension/truncation behavior.
Spin flags or fields changed by asynchronous callbacks should be modeled as volatile when the decompiled function polls them.
Data-symbol typing in symbols.txt can matter: lbl_8040806C was marked data:byte to match its use as a u8 lookup table.
When a compiler emits struct-zeroing or clearing patterns that do not map cleanly to memset, a local inline zeroing helper may be a practical matching tactic.
Callback-like functions may have unused parameters; keeping the full observed signature, as with HSD_SynthResetStreamCounters, can be necessary even when the body ignores the parameters.
AX/HSD synth work may require matching both code and data layout together: static mix buffers, lookup tables, and padding were part of this match set.
If a struct field appears semantically mis-typed, document the mismatch and cast locally rather than broadening the change without stronger evidence.
naming_conventions: Unknown HSD synth functions kept address-based names such as HSD_Synth_80388E08 and HSD_Synth_8038B5AC.
Known public synth APIs use HSD_SynthSFX* names, e.g. HSD_SynthSFXKeyOff and HSD_SynthSFXUpdateMix.
Unknown data symbols retain lbl_8040xxxx or HSD_Synth_804Dxxxx address-based names.
Local matching helpers use descriptive lower/camel names such as freeVoices, stopRange, my_memzero, and HSD_Synth_8038B5AC_inline.
The PR did not rename unknown functions beyond the evidence in the diff; most newly matched unknowns remained address-based.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added PAD_STACK(0x10) in HSD_SynthSFXKeyOff and PAD_STACK(8) in HSD_Synth_8038B5AC to reproduce expected stack layout.
Used static inline helper extraction to influence inlining and control-flow shape.
Used an explicit byte table lbl_8040806C for ITD shift lookup instead of deriving values algorithmically.
Used my_memzero with int stores over sizeof(AXPBMIX), likely to match the original clearing loop more closely than a library memset call.
Used volatile u8 for HSD_Synth_804D7778 to preserve a busy-wait loop over a global flag.
Used sqrtf_accurate and exact float constants such as 0.003921569F, 32767.0F, and 23169.768F in HSD_SynthSFXUpdateMix.
Kept interrupt boundaries with OSDisableInterrupts and OSRestoreInterrupts around synth node list/volume update manipulation.
Adjusted function signatures to u8/bool where necessary to match parameter handling.

review_feedback.summary: Bot reported GALE01 matched code increased to 41.84%, +0.06% and +2304 bytes, with 4 new full matches: HSD_SynthSFXUpdateMix (+1200), HSD_Synth_8038B5AC (+624), HSD_SynthSFXKeyOff (+308), and HSD_Synth_80388E08 (+172).
No human review comments or PR body rationale were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that resolve the TODO about AXVPB prev needing a signed int type instead of a pointer.
Search for additional HSD synth stream functions near HSD_Synth_8038AD74, HSD_Synth_8038ADD0, and HSD_Synth_8038B120.
Look for other PRs that mark unknown data tables as data:byte in config/GALE01/symbols.txt.
Search for uses of volatile globals in callback/spin-wait matching across sysdolphin baselib.
Compare later synth.h changes to see whether HSD_SynthPStreamHeaderCallback and HSD_SynthResetStreamCounters signatures were further refined.
